data.title: Stock index futures rise after trading resumes post CME's disruption
data.description:
Stock index futures edged higher on Friday as trading resumed following CME Group’s (CME) temporary halt caused by a cooling issue at data centers.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, while Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) were flat.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.00%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.48%.

Futures trading for stock indices, commodities, and Treasuries was halted early on Friday due to a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers. The Chicago Mercantile Exchange is the largest derivatives marketplace, with contracts ranging from equities, commodities, bonds and beyond.

U.S. markets are coming off the Thanksgiving holiday and will operate on a shorter schedule today, closing at 1 p.m. ET for Black Friday.

"Meanwhile in the U.S., both equity and bond markets were closed for Thanksgiving, but the futures markets that were opened showed barely any movement either. So around the time of the European close, futures on the S&P 500 were completely flat, and Treasury futures indicated that yields would only rise by up to a basis point across the curve," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

The economic calendar is light, with mainly the Fed balance sheet slated to land later in the day.

data.title: China Investment Corp. close to finalizing ~$1B U.S. private equity stake sale - report
data.description:
China Investment Corp., a sovereign wealth fund that manages part of China's foreign exchange reserves, is close to finalizing the sale of its ~$1B U.S. private equity stake, people familiar with the matter told Bloomberg News.

France-based private equity investment firm Ardian SAS is among final bidders for the portfolio, which includes stakes managed by Carlyle Group (CG), Hellman & Friedman, and Welsh Carson Anderson & Stowe, insiders reportedly said.

The $1.3T sovereign wealth fund is said to be reducing exposure to U.S. private markets and diversifying away from illiquid dollar assets.

Earlier in 2025, the Chinese fund was seeking to offload $1B in fund positions with firms including Carlyle (CG), KKR (KKR), and TPG (TPG). The process resumed after a temporary pause in the deal, the November 28 report noted.

data.title: Canada’s GDP grows 0.6% in Q3
data.description:
The Gross Domestic Product (GDP) in Canada expanded 0.6% in the third quarter of 2025 over the previous quarter. 

GDP growth annualized in Canada increased to 2.6% in the third quarter of 2025 from -1.80% in the second quarter of 2025.

data.title: GameStop looks for buzz with a wild 'trade anything' promotion
data.description:
GameStop (GME) is hosting a "Trade Anything Day" on December 6. The promotional event will allow customers to bring almost any physical item to a GameStop (GME) store and trade it in for $5 in store credit. "We look forward to seeing the whackiest items you can bring!," updated the retailer. 

The promotion appears designed to encourage foot traffic and engagement, expanding GameStop's (GME) usual buyback format for one day only. The event has already attracted some buzz on social media from potential customers and purported employees.

Participants may only bring one item per customer, and the object must fit within a 20-by-20-by-20-inch box. Store employees reserve the right to reject items based on company guidelines. Certain items are specifically excluded or prohibited, such as hazardous materials, weapons, lithium-ion batteries, most computers and accessories, certain electronics, alcohol, tobacco, drugs, any live or dead animals (except taxidermy), jewelry, gift cards, currency, sexually explicit items, and objects resembling body parts. 

GameStop's (GME) current strategy is built around high-margin collectibles, a leaner retail footprint, and disciplined capital allocation. While GameStop (GME) has a very large cash hoard ($8.7 billion at the end of Q2), it has not made any strategic investments and acquisitions. The Grapevine, Texas-based company is due to report Q3 earnings on December 9 and update on its cash position. 

Shares of GameStop (GME) were up 2.9% in premarket trading to $22.25. The stock is down about 30% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Wearable Devices secures $5.68M through warrant inducement deal with institutional investor
data.description:
Wearable Devices (WLDS) has announced its entry into a warrant inducement agreement with an existing institutional investor of the company for the immediate exercise of warrants to purchase up to 3,322,000 ordinary shares at an exercise price of $1.71 per share for gross proceeds of ~$5.68 million.

The company intends to use the net proceeds from the warrant inducement transaction for working capital and other general corporate purposes.

The stock is up +4.84% premarket at $1.95.

data.title: Comcast CEO plans to join the next round of bidding for WBD's streaming and studio ops: NYP
data.description:
Comcast CEO (CMCSA) Brian Roberts is planning to join a second round of bidding for Warner Bros. Discovery next week for just its studio and streaming businesses, according to a report by the New York Post on Friday, citing a source.

The report, citing sources, said Roberts is weighing a potential offer that could be higher than that of rivals, at around $27 or $28 a share. Roberts and his team have not come up with a final number, but if they bid, it would be a respectable premium to the roughly $25 a share already placed by Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for the entire company.

Comcast's bid would also likely surpass the first-round bid made by Netflix (NFLX), which is also vying for the studio and streaming pieces of WBD, the report said, citing sources. 

Sources told NYP that Roberts knows he needs to play hardball to win. He’s making a run at WBD despite extreme reluctance from the Trump administration to approve anything Roberts touches, given his long stewardship of the Trump-hating cable channel MS NOW, the report said.

Roberts is said to believe he has no choice due to Comcast’s challenges: a laggard streaming service in Peacock, a second-place network in NBC, and a smallish studio on top of lots of debt as he spins off MS NOW and other cable channels into a new company.

The thinking is that with a leading bid, Roberts might be able to get the green light from the WBD board and then successfully challenge in federal court any rebuttal from the Trump DOJ antitrust department, arguing that in the age of multiple streaming services and content being offered on YouTube and social media, no significant antitrust issues exist with his offer, the report said.

data.title: TotalEnergies, Repsol in talks to combine U.K. upstream assets - report
data.description:
France's TotalEnergies (TTE) and Spain's Repsol (OTCQX:REPYF) (OTCQX:REPYY) are in advanced talks to merge their oil and gas exploration and production businesses in the U.K. North Sea, Spanish business newspaper Cinco Dias reported late Wednesday.

Repsol (OTCQX:REPYF) (OTCQX:REPYY) recently established a U.K. joint venture with Neo Energy, and TotalEnergies (TTE) could join the business by contributing some of its assets, according to the report.

Bloomberg recently reported that Repsol (OTCQX:REPYF) (OTCQX:REPYY) is considering a reverse merger of its upstream unit with potential U.S.-listed partners including APA Corp., as the Spanish company seeks ways to list the business on the NYSE.

Repsol (OTCQX:REPYF) (OTCQX:REPYY) also reportedly has held initial talks with other potential merger partners, and is studying various options for the business including an IPO or stake sale.

data.title: German warehouse employees strike on Black Friday as part of 'Make Amazon Pay' day
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) employees at nine German warehouses were on strike on Black Friday, seeking a collective bargaining agreement and better wages from the e-commerce giant, Reuters reported Friday.

Services union Verdi says approximately 3,000 German workers were on strike at Amazon’s (AMZN) facilities, including one of its biggest fulfilment centers at Bad Hersfeld.

While the work stoppage aims to disrupt sales on the one of the biggest retail day of the holiday season, Amazon (AMZN) said the strike will have no impact on orders thanks to a staff of 40,000.

The German strikes are part of a larger “Make Amazon Pay” campaign across the globe by the UNI Global Union.

The protests will include union members, human-rights organizations, environmentalist groups, and tech employees on Black Friday to Cyber Monday to draw attention to claims that Amazon (AMZN) is fueling “an unholy alliance between Big Tech and the far right.”

“Amazon is remaking the global economy in its own image – extractive, exploitive and authoritarian,” the group states on its “Make Amazon Pay” website.

Seeking Alpha has contacted Amazon (AMZN) for a response.  

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Hanstone Gold appoints interim CEO
data.description:
Hanstone Gold (OTCPK:HANCF) (HANS:CA) announced on Friday the appointment of Robert Quinn as interim CEO.

Quinn replaces Raymond Marks.

data.title: North Peak Resources appoints Jim O'Neill as CFO
data.description:
• North Peak Resources (NPR:CA) has appointed Jim O'Neill as Chief Financial Officer and Corporate Secretary of the company, with immediate effect.
  • O'Neill succeeds Andrew Dunlop, who has stepped down from those positions with the Company.
  • O'Neill has over 30 years of experience as a finance executive with multinational businesses operational in Canada, USA, the UK, Turkey, and Kenya, the company said.
  • Source: Press Release
